title: FHA Down Payment Assistance in Florida (2026) | Programs & Eligibility
description: How to combine FHA loans with Florida down payment assistance — Hometown Heroes, Florida Assist, and local SHIP programs. Learn eligibility and how DPA covers your down payment.
keywords: fha down payment assistance florida, florida down payment assistance, fha loan down payment help florida, florida hometown heroes fha

Home/FHA Down Payment Assistance Florida
FHA Down Payment Assistance Florida

FHA down payment assistance in Florida

Short on cash to close? Florida down payment assistance programs can pair with your FHA loan to cover your down payment and closing costs — here's how it works.

Get My Free Rate QuoteCall __PHONE_DISPLAY__
Covers down paymentPairs with FHAIncome limits applyStatewide & local programs

Get your free FHA quote

No obligation. See your rate & payment in minutes.

🔒 Your information is secure. By submitting you agree to be contacted about FHA loan options. This is not a commitment to lend.

Thank you!

An FHA mortgage specialist will reach out shortly with your personalized Florida rate quote.

Down Payment Help

FHA down payment assistance in Florida

The down payment is the single biggest hurdle for most Florida buyers — not the monthly payment. Down payment assistance (DPA) closes that gap. Florida runs one of the most generous DPA programs in the country, and it pairs directly with an FHA loan, so qualified buyers can get into a home with little or nothing out of pocket.

Here is what is actually available in Florida, who qualifies, and how the money works when you sell or refinance later.

Hometown HeroesUp to $35,000

Down payment + closing

Interest rate0%

No interest charged

Monthly payment$0

Deferred second mortgage

Eligible jobs50+

Plus open categories

Hometown Heroes

The program most Florida buyers should know

Florida Hometown Heroes — the flagship program

Administered by Florida Housing, Hometown Heroes provides up to 5% of your first mortgage amount, capped at $35,000 (minimum $10,000), to use toward your down payment and closing costs. It comes as a 0% interest, non-amortizing second mortgage — you make no monthly payments on it. You repay the original amount only when you sell, refinance, transfer, or pay off the first mortgage. It is not forgivable, but it never accrues interest.

Who qualifies

  • First-time buyer — or simply no homeownership in the past three years (this requirement is waived for veterans).
  • Full-time employment (35+ hours/week) with a Florida-based employer in an eligible occupation — teachers, nurses, first responders, and 50+ fields (veterans are exempt from the occupation rule).
  • Income within county limits — up to 150% of area median income, roughly $142,950–$195,450 depending on the county.
  • Credit score generally 640+ (660 for manufactured homes).
  • Homebuyer education course completed by the primary borrower (waived for veterans using a VA loan).
  • The home must be your primary residence — no vacation or investment properties.
Your Options

Ways to cover your down payment

Hometown Heroes is the headliner, but it is not the only source of help. Here is how the common Florida options compare:

SourceHow muchRepaymentBest for
Florida Hometown HeroesUp to $35,0000%, deferred until sale/refiEligible workers, first-time buyers
County SHIP fundsVaries by countyOften partially or fully forgivableLower-income buyers in participating counties
Family gift fundsNo limit (FHA)None — it’s a giftBuyers with family able to help

In some counties you can stack Hometown Heroes with local SHIP funds, pushing total assistance past $50,000. Availability depends on your county and remaining allocations.

Timing

Why getting pre-approved early matters

One thing to know: funding runs out

Hometown Heroes funding is limited and released in rounds on a first-come, first-served basis — past rounds have been exhausted in a matter of weeks. You cannot reserve funds until you are both pre-approved and under contract on a home. The practical takeaway: get pre-approved now, even before funding is open, so you are ready to reserve the moment a round opens.

How To Get It

Securing assistance, step by step

01

Get pre-approved

We confirm your FHA loan eligibility and check which assistance programs you qualify for based on your job, income, and county.

02

Complete homebuyer education

Finish the required course (online and quick). Veterans using a VA loan are exempt.

03

Go under contract

Find your home and get an accepted offer — the trigger that lets us reserve your assistance funds.

04

Reserve & close

We reserve the assistance, finalize your FHA loan, and the funds are applied at closing toward your down payment and costs.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

How much down payment assistance can I get in Florida?+
Through Florida Hometown Heroes, up to 5% of your first mortgage amount, capped at $35,000 (minimum $10,000). In some counties you may stack it with local SHIP funds for more.
Do I have to pay the assistance back?+
Hometown Heroes is a 0% interest, deferred second mortgage. You make no monthly payments, and you repay the original amount only when you sell, refinance, transfer, or pay off your first mortgage. It is not forgivable, but it never charges interest.
Who is eligible for Hometown Heroes?+
Generally: a first-time buyer (or no ownership in the past three years), employed full-time by a Florida employer in an eligible occupation, with income under your county’s limit and a credit score around 640+. Veterans are exempt from the first-time-buyer and occupation requirements.
What jobs qualify?+
More than 50 occupations qualify, including teachers, nurses and healthcare workers, law enforcement, firefighters and other first responders, and many more community roles. We can confirm whether your specific job qualifies.
What are the income limits?+
They are set by county at up to 150% of area median income — roughly $142,950 to $195,450 in 2026 depending on where you buy and your household size.
Can I combine assistance with an FHA loan?+
Yes. Hometown Heroes is designed to pair with FHA (and other) first mortgages, and the assistance is applied at closing toward your down payment and closing costs.
Is the program always open?+
No. Funding is released in limited rounds and runs out quickly. You must be pre-approved and under contract to reserve funds, so it pays to get pre-approved before a round opens.
Can I still get help if I don’t qualify for Hometown Heroes?+
Often yes. County SHIP programs and gift funds are alternatives, and we can review which options fit your situation.
